title: Re-run regression suite after QA fixes
description:
Summary:
Run the final automated regression pass after shell/overlay and graph/editor QA fixes are complete.

Acceptance criteria:
- Jest, production build, and Cypress regression coverage pass or have documented environment limitations with equivalent targeted coverage.
- No P0 redesign regression remains open.
- Results are recorded for release handoff.

Design notes:
- This is the release gate for retiring Material UI, not the first discovery pass.

Edge cases:
- Regressions fixed in one area must not re-break another migrated surface.

Testing strategy:
- Re-run failed specs from the first pass, then run the broadest practical full suite.

title: Fix toolbar menu and dialog regressions found during QA
description:
## Summary
Address any shell, toolbar, menu, dialog, drawer, snackbar, or form regressions discovered during final QA.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Complete the shell, overlay, form, graph/editor, and dependency-cleanup migration tasks.
2. Run the first full automated regression pass in gviz-4wn.3.
3. Reproduce any failures that involve toolbar, menus, dialogs, drawers, snackbar, forms, focus, escape, selectors, or persisted setting behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every failing shell/overlay test has a fix or a clearly justified test update for equivalent behavior.
- Escape, focus restoration, click handlers, IDs, and persisted setting flows pass after fixes.
- No fix reintroduces MUI dependencies.

## Design Notes
- Keep fixes behavior-first; visual refinements can be follow-up tasks unless they block usability.

## Edge Cases
- Nested confirmations, portaled menus, narrow toolbar, and snackbar offset.

## Testing Strategy
- Rerun the failing specs plus shell/overlay smoke checks.

title: Fix graph and editor regressions found during QA
description:
## Summary
Address any graph canvas, SVG rendering, DOT editor, selection, pan/zoom, transition, or visual editing regressions discovered during final QA.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Complete the shell, overlay, form, graph/editor, and dependency-cleanup migration tasks.
2. Run the first full automated regression pass in gviz-4wn.3.
3. Reproduce any failures that involve DOT editing, graph rendering, visual editing, generated SVG, selection, pan/zoom, transitions, fullscreen, or backend sync behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every failing graph/editor test has a fix or a clearly justified test update for equivalent behavior.
- Graph parsing, mutation, selection, deletion, undo/redo, and generated SVG IDs/classes remain stable.
- No fix changes DOT semantics or backend sync contracts.

## Design Notes
- Keep graph/editor content visually primary and avoid CSS fixes that globally affect generated SVG unexpectedly.

## Edge Cases
- Large graphs, malformed DOT, fullscreen, transition animation, and selected edges with markers.

## Testing Strategy
- Rerun failing specs plus rendering, text_editor, pan_and_zoom, select/delete, draw_edge, transition, and fullscreen_graph specs.

title: Remove obsolete dependencies and lockfile entries
description:
Summary:
Remove unused direct dependencies from package.json and regenerate the lockfile after MUI migration.

Acceptance criteria:
- Direct dependencies no longer include unused @mui/material, @mui/icons-material, @mui/codemod, @emotion/react, @emotion/styled, tss-react, or typeface-roboto.
- Base UI and any chosen icon/font packages remain declared.
- Lockfile is updated through the package manager, not edited manually.

Design notes:
- Dependency removal should happen only after source imports are gone.

Edge cases:
- Some packages may remain transitive; do not fight the lockfile if another dependency needs them.

Testing strategy:
- Run npm install or npm prune as appropriate, then npm test -- --watchAll=false and npm run build.

title: Run full automated regression suite
description:
Summary:
Run the complete automated test/build suite after dependency cleanup and triage the result.

Acceptance criteria:
- Jest tests are run and pass, or failures are captured with concrete follow-up bug tasks.
- Production build is run and passes, or failures are captured with concrete follow-up bug tasks.
- Cypress specs are run, or any environment-only limitation is documented with the targeted specs that did run.
- Test output is attached to the handoff notes for the redesign branch.

Design notes:
- This is the first full regression pass after Material UI removal; failures should unblock focused bug-fix tasks rather than be hidden.

Edge cases:
- Cypress may require backend/dev server orchestration and browser availability.
- Graphviz build step can emit known warnings; distinguish warnings from failures.

Testing strategy:
- Run npm test -- --watchAll=false, npm run build, npm run backend/start:local as needed, and npm run integration-test or targeted Cypress specs.

title: Retire Material UI after regression proof
description:
## Summary
Remove Material UI, tss-react, Roboto, and obsolete styling paths only after migrated UI surfaces pass regression checks.

## Success Criteria
- package.json and lockfile no longer include unused Material UI, Emotion, tss-react, @mui/icons-material, @mui/codemod, or typeface-roboto dependencies after verification confirms they are unused.
- rg finds no @mui, tss-react/mui, withStyles, useTheme, CssBaseline, ThemeProvider, or typeface-roboto imports in application source.
- Full automated regression suite and build pass before this epic is closed.

## Design Notes
- Dependency removal is the final proof that the app owns its interface layer.

## Edge Cases
- Emotion may still be transitive; only remove direct dependencies that are no longer imported.
- Build output and generated files should follow repo policy and avoid unrelated churn.

## Testing Strategy
- Run npm test -- --watchAll=false, npm run build, and Cypress regression suite or documented targeted subset if the full suite is impractical locally.

title: Remove residual MUI and tss imports
description:
Summary:
Search the source tree and remove any remaining direct references to Material UI, MUI icons, tss-react, and theme wrappers after component migration.

Acceptance criteria:
- rg reports no direct source imports from @mui/material, @mui/icons-material, tss-react/mui, or typeface-roboto.
- Any obsolete wrapper files are deleted or simplified.
- No functional code is changed outside UI import/styling cleanup.

Design notes:
- Cleanup should be mechanical and scoped.

Edge cases:
- GitHubIcon replacement and withRoot removal may leave dead imports or exports.

Testing strategy:
- Run lint/search checks and npm run build.

title: Verify graph and editor workflows end to end
description:
Summary:
Run the full graph/editor workflow set after work surface migration.

Acceptance criteria:
- DOT edit/render loop, insert node, draw edge, select/deselect, delete, undo/redo, pan/zoom, fullscreen, transition settings, and parser errors pass automated or documented manual checks.
- Any regression is filed or fixed before Material UI removal begins.
- No work surface component still imports MUI/tss-react after migration tasks complete.

Design notes:
- Treat any behavior drift as a blocker, even if the visual design looks complete.

Edge cases:
- Large DOT documents, malformed DOT, slow render holdoff, backend sync updates, and fullscreen interactions.

Testing strategy:
- Run targeted Cypress specs for graph/editor flows, then include them in full regression.

title: Preserve SVG graph selection and marker styling
description:
Summary:
Confirm the new global CSS and graph chrome do not break d3-graphviz generated SVG styling or selection markers.

Acceptance criteria:
- Node and edge selection markings remain visible and match current behavior.
- CSS reset does not alter generated SVG text, paths, polygons, arrowheads, or marker colors unexpectedly.
- Graph mutations still produce expected SVG IDs/classes for Cypress helpers.

Design notes:
- App CSS must be scoped carefully around generated graph SVG.

Edge cases:
- Dark fill colors, selected edges with arrow markers, transition animation, and default Graphviz colors.

Testing strategy:
- Run select_deselect, select_delete, draw_edge, rendering, and transition specs.

title: Verify overlay focus escape and scroll locking
description:
Summary:
Run focused checks for all migrated overlays and forms.

Acceptance criteria:
- Escape closes active overlays as before.
- Focus trap/restoration works for dialog, nested confirmation, menu, drawer, and snackbar cases.
- Body/page scroll locking does not break graph pan/zoom or editor scrolling.
- Cypress specs covering overlays pass or are updated for equivalent stable selectors.

Design notes:
- Behavior correctness is more important than visual polish in this task.

Edge cases:
- Nested confirmation from open-from-browser, dialog opened from menu, graph fullscreen plus overlay, and editor focus before opening settings.

Testing strategy:
- Run settings, browser_save_and_open, help, fullscreen_graph, pan_and_zoom, and smoke overlay specs.

title: Verify shell workflows against Cypress
description:
Summary:
Run and repair shell/navigation regressions introduced during shell migration.

Acceptance criteria:
- Relevant Cypress specs pass for toolbar, menus, fullscreen, GitHub link, settings entry, help entry, and graph pan/zoom entry points.
- Any selector updates are committed with the component changes that require them.
- No shell task remains accepted with known broken workflows.

Design notes:
- Test failures should be treated as behavior regressions unless proven to be stale Material-specific assertions.

Edge cases:
- Flaky graph rendering waits and portaled overlay timing.

Testing strategy:
- Run targeted Cypress specs first, then include shell smoke specs in full regression later.

title: Build form and table primitives
description:
Summary:
Create app-owned fields for input, number input, select, switch, checkbox, radio group, label/helper text, table, sortable header, and inline adornment.

Acceptance criteria:
- Primitives can replace SettingsDialog, FormatDrawer, ColorPicker, SaveAsToBrowserDialog, ExportAsSvgDialog, and OpenFromBrowserDialog controls.
- Inputs preserve current value, change, blur, enter, and validation behavior.
- Form controls expose accessible names and helper text relationships.

Design notes:
- Forms should be compact and utilitarian, with visible grouping and direct labels.
- State should be apparent without relying on Material color conventions.

Edge cases:
- Numeric fields currently receive strings from localStorage and event.target.value; preserve these handler contracts.
- Switches/checkboxes must preserve event.target.checked semantics.

Testing strategy:
- Run settings, transition, text editor, and format drawer workflows after adoption.

title: Build toolbar and button primitives
description:
Summary:
Create app-owned primitives for icon buttons, text buttons, toolbar groups, tooltips, disabled states, and external links.

Acceptance criteria:
- Primitives cover current toolbar actions: menu, new, open, save, undo, redo, zoom controls, insert, node format, edge format, settings, GitHub, and help.
- Components preserve aria-labels, IDs, click handlers, disabled behavior, and keyboard focus behavior.
- Styling uses tokens and app CSS, not MUI or tss-react.

Design notes:
- Buttons should feel like precise editor controls: compact, rectangular, high-contrast, and readable.
- Use familiar iconography for icon-only actions, with accessible labels.

Edge cases:
- GitHub external link must remain a link with the same href.
- Toolbar overflow/narrow width must not hide core graph/editor actions without an alternate path.

Testing strategy:
- Run toolbar-related Cypress specs after ButtonAppBar migration.

title: Build overlay and positioning primitives
description:
Summary:
Create wrappers around Base UI overlay primitives for dialogs, alert dialogs, drawers, menus, popovers, tooltips, and toast/snackbar behavior.

Acceptance criteria:
- Wrappers provide consistent portal root handling, backdrop, escape handling, focus trap/restoration, close affordance, and z-index layering.
- Dialog, drawer, menu, tooltip, and snackbar replacements can use the same primitive conventions.
- Existing IDs can be placed on stable app-owned nodes.

Design notes:
- Brutalist overlays should be plain, structural, and content-first: strong borders, no Material shadows, compact titles, direct actions.

Edge cases:
- Graph fullscreen and dialogs must layer correctly.
- Escape should close the active overlay without corrupting editor focus state.
- Body scroll locking must not interfere with graph pan/zoom.

Testing strategy:
- Verify settings dialog, help menu, and snackbar behavior after first adoption.

title: Load custom font assets and global reset
description:
Summary:
Replace typeface-roboto with custom font loading and a reset that supports the brutalist/minimal direction.

Acceptance criteria:
- Roboto import is removed from src/pages/index.js once replacement typography is in place.
- Global CSS defines app font families, baseline line-height, focus outline, button/input inheritance, and portal stacking context.
- Font loading strategy is documented and does not block the editor from rendering.

Design notes:
- Use a distinctive display/control font and a legible editor-adjacent body font; avoid Roboto, Arial, Inter, Open Sans, and default system fallback as the primary identity.
- Typography must keep dense toolbars and dialogs readable.

Edge cases:
- Ace editor font size settings must continue to control editor content.
- Graph SVG labels are generated by Graphviz and should not be inadvertently restyled unless explicitly scoped.

Testing strategy:
- Run Cypress text editor font-size tests.
- Manually verify default, settings dialog, and narrow toolbar views.

title: Define brutalist minimal design tokens
description:
Summary:
Create app-owned CSS variables for spacing, borders, color, typography, z-index, and motion.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tokens cover app shell, toolbar, panes, overlays, controls, tables, focus rings, disabled states, graph chrome, and editor chrome.
- Token names describe intent rather than source library components.
- Tokens avoid Material UI shadows/elevation and one-note palettes.

Design notes:
- Use strong boundaries, compact rhythm, flat planes, explicit state changes, and restrained accent colors.
- Keep graph content visually primary; UI chrome should frame the work without competing with it.

Edge cases:
- Tokens must account for fullscreen mode and updated snackbar offset.
- Motion tokens must respect reduced-motion preferences.

Testing strategy:
- Verify tokens are consumed by at least one primitive and one shell surface before broad rollout.

title: Build custom brutalist UI foundation
description:
## Summary
Replace the Material UI theme/styling foundation with a custom, app-owned design system built on Base UI primitives, CSS, and custom fonts.

## Success Criteria
- The foundation provides tokens, global CSS, font loading, layout primitives, interaction states, and Base UI wrappers for the component families used by the app.
- New components are unstyled-by-default at the library layer and styled through app-owned CSS.
- Material UI and tss-react are not used for any new UI work after this foundation lands.

## Design Notes
- Direction: brutalist/minimal editor for graph work, with high contrast, strict grid, compact controls, obvious borders, direct labels, and custom typography.
- Avoid Material Design shadows, rounded cards, Roboto, generic gradients, and decorative chrome.
- Use Base UI because it is headless/unstyled and leaves CSS control in the app.

## Edge Cases
- Portals must layer correctly above the graph canvas and editor.
- CSS reset must not break Ace editor internals, d3-graphviz SVG output, or react-color widgets.

## Testing Strategy
- Build primitives with small integration examples in migrated components rather than isolated unused code.
- Run Jest/build after global CSS and dependency changes.

title: Install Base UI dependency behind foundation branch
description:
Summary:
Add the current Base UI React package and verify it works with React 18 and Create React App in this repository.

Acceptance criteria:
- package.json and lockfile include the selected Base UI package.
- Installation uses the current official package name @base-ui/react unless implementation research proves a different package is required.
- A minimal import from one Base UI primitive compiles in CRA.

Design notes:
- Base UI should provide accessible behavior while all styling stays in app-owned CSS.
- Do not introduce Tailwind or another styling framework for this migration.

Edge cases:
- Watch for ESM/CJS compatibility with react-scripts 5.
- Avoid adding duplicate headless libraries for the same primitive family.

Testing strategy:
- Run npm install, npm test -- --watchAll=false or targeted Jest, and npm run build after dependency changes.

title: Harden stable selector contract
description:
Summary:
Confirm the selector and accessibility contract that redesigned components must preserve.

Acceptance criteria:
- Existing IDs used by Cypress commands remain listed as required compatibility selectors.
- Any replacement selector is mapped one-for-one and updated in cypress/support/commands.js in the same task that changes the DOM.
- Interactive controls retain accessible names at least as specific as the current aria-labels and visible labels.

Design notes:
- Base UI internals may change wrapper markup; app-owned IDs should live on stable elements that reflect user actions.
- Avoid coupling tests to generated component structure.

Edge cases:
- Portaled dialogs and menus must still be discoverable by tests.
- Controls nested inside Base UI parts must expose stable click targets.

Testing strategy:
- Run Cypress specs that exercise toolbar, main menu, settings, and dialogs after selector changes.

title: Define no-functionality-change checklist
description:
Summary:
Create the working checklist each migration slice must satisfy before it is considered done.

Acceptance criteria:
- Checklist states that business logic, graph parsing, graph mutation, persistence keys, backend RPC/SSE contracts, and Cypress workflow outcomes must remain unchanged.
- Checklist requires before/after verification for each migrated component.
- Checklist defines when a visual-only change is allowed and when behavior review is required.

Design notes:
- The checklist should be short enough to use during every 5-30 minute task.
- The brutalist/minimal design direction must be constrained by the behavior contract.

Edge cases:
- Explicitly cover keyboard escape handling, focus restoration, and fullscreen exit behavior.

Testing strategy:
- Validate the checklist against at least one shell component and one dialog component before broad migration.

title: Audit current MUI touchpoints and behavior owners
description:
Summary:
Build a migration inventory for every file importing @mui/material, @mui/icons-material, tss-react/mui, typeface-roboto, or theme helpers.

Acceptance criteria:
- Inventory lists the source file, MUI components/hooks used, current DOM IDs/classes relied on by Cypress, and owning user workflow.
- Inventory identifies files with high behavior risk: src/pages/index.js, Graph.js, TextEditor.js, SettingsDialog.js, FormatDrawer.js, OpenFromBrowserDialog.js, and ButtonAppBar.js.
- Inventory separates purely visual wrappers from components that manage focus, portals, selection, persistence, or keyboard behavior.

Design notes:
- Use this audit to plan adapter boundaries, not to redesign functionality.
- Keep the app's graph/editor model untouched.

Edge cases:
- Include indirect MUI dependence through withRoot and SvgIcon.
- Include visual state currently encoded as MUI elevation, transitions, or theme spacing.

Testing strategy:
- Cross-check inventory against rg results for @mui, tss-react, typeface-roboto, useTheme, withStyles, and CssBaseline.

title: Capture current interaction baselines
description:
Summary:
Record the current behavior of the editor before UI replacement begins.

Acceptance criteria:
- Baseline notes cover initial render, DOT edit/render loop, insert node, draw edge, selection/delete, undo/redo, pan/zoom, fullscreen, settings, browser save/open, export URL, export SVG, help menu, keyboard shortcuts, mouse operations, and about dialog.
- Baseline includes current viewport behavior for desktop and narrow widths.
- Any existing flaky test or hard-to-observe behavior is called out with reproduction steps.

Design notes:
- Baseline is functional, not aesthetic. Screenshots can support layout comparison but must not become approval for changing workflows.

Edge cases:
- Include persisted settings and projects from localStorage.
- Include graph transition settings because animations can hide behavior regressions.

Testing strategy:
- Run representative Cypress specs and capture pass/fail state.
- Use manual notes only where automated coverage is missing or unreliable.

title: Preserve existing editor behavior during UI rewrite
description:
## Summary
Create a behavior-first safety net before replacing Material UI. The redesign must not change DOT editing, graph rendering, visual editing, persistence, export/import, settings, keyboard shortcuts, backend sync, or existing Cypress-facing selectors.

## Success Criteria
- Every migration task can point to a baseline behavior source: current code, existing Cypress spec, new smoke check, or explicit manual checklist item.
- Existing public IDs and test helpers remain stable unless a task explicitly updates tests for semantically equivalent markup.
- No visual redesign work lands without a way to prove graph/editor behavior stayed intact.

## Design Notes
- Treat the app as an expert tool for editing Graphviz/DOT, not a marketing surface.
- Preserve dense, command-oriented workflows while changing only presentation and component internals.
- Brutalist/minimal direction should increase clarity through typography, grid, contrast, and restraint rather than adding new flows.

## Edge Cases
- LocalStorage restoration, URL import, export dialogs, backend SSE sync, focus handoff between panes, and fullscreen graph mode are all part of the protected behavior surface.

## Testing Strategy
- Run existing Jest/Cypress coverage before component swaps.
- Add targeted smoke coverage for view states that currently lack stable regression checks.
- Compare behavior before and after each migration slice rather than waiting for the final removal.

title: Audit accessibility and keyboard behavior
description:
Summary:
Perform a final accessibility and keyboard audit of the redesigned interface.

Acceptance criteria:
- Toolbar, menus, dialogs, drawers, settings controls, tables, and graph fullscreen controls are keyboard reachable.
- Focus indicators are visible and consistent with the custom design system.
- Dialog labels/descriptions, form labels/helper text, and icon-only button labels are present.
- Reduced motion preference is respected for app-authored motion.

Design notes:
- Brutalist/minimal styling should make focus and state more explicit, not less.

Edge cases:
- Portaled overlays, nested confirmations, disabled controls, and hidden color pickers.

Testing strategy:
- Manual keyboard pass plus targeted Cypress assertions for focus/escape where practical.

title: Re-skin color picker shell
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI FormControl/Input usage in ColorPicker.js while preserving react-color behavior and payloads.

Acceptance criteria:
- Color text input and picker still update color values exactly as before.
- IDs and event handlers used by FormatDrawer remain stable.
- Color picker visibility controlled by drawer switches is unchanged.

Design notes:
- Use a simple swatch/input pairing that fits the inspector drawer.

Edge cases:
- Empty color, invalid color text, rapid picker changes, and separate color/fillcolor instances.

Testing strategy:
- Manually verify node and edge color/fillcolor changes and run format-related specs.

title: Re-skin graph viewport controls and loading state
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI Fade, CircularProgress, IconButton, fullscreen icons, and tss styles in Graph.js with custom primitives.

Acceptance criteria:
- Canvas rendering, loading state, fullscreen open/close, zoom registration, pan/zoom, selection, deletion, drag selection, and edge drawing behavior are unchanged.
- Graph control IDs remain stable, including fullscreen.
- Loading state is accessible and visually aligned with the custom system.

Design notes:
- Graph chrome should be minimal and should not obscure SVG content.

Edge cases:
- Slow graph render, transition animation, fullscreen mode, mouse drag interactions, and selected component styling.

Testing strategy:
- Run rendering, fullscreen_graph, pan_and_zoom, select_deselect, select_delete, draw_edge, and transition specs.

title: Redesign graph and editor work surfaces
description:
## Summary
Apply the new visual language to the graph canvas, text editor chrome, preview surfaces, color picker shell, and graph controls without touching graph parsing or mutation behavior.

## Success Criteria
- Graph rendering, selection, pan/zoom, visual editing, DOT editing, error display, previews, color picking, and fullscreen behavior match current workflows.
- MUI/tss-react imports are removed from Graph.js, TextEditor.js, DotSrcPreview.js, SvgPreview.js, ColorPicker.js, and GitHubIcon.js where applicable.
- Third-party internals for Ace, d3-graphviz, and react-color remain stable.

## Design Notes
- The graph and DOT editor are the product. UI chrome should make them feel precise and controlled.

## Edge Cases
- Generated SVG content must not be globally restyled by the new CSS system.

## Testing Strategy
- Run rendering, text_editor, pan_and_zoom, select/deselect, select_delete, draw_edge, transition, fullscreen_graph, and color/format checks.

title: Re-skin text editor chrome and error affordance
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI IconButton/ErrorOutline and tss styles in TextEditor.js with app primitives while leaving Ace editor behavior untouched.

Acceptance criteria:
- Text editor wrapper, error button, tooltip trigger, font-size setting, tab-size setting, and parser error highlighting behave as before.
- Existing IDs including text-editor-wrapper, text-editor, and error-button remain stable.
- Ace internal CSS is not overwritten unexpectedly.

Design notes:
- Editor chrome should be restrained and dense, with an unmistakable error affordance.

Edge cases:
- Error tooltip positioning, long syntax errors, very large DOT sources, and font size changes.

Testing strategy:
- Run text_editor spec, especially parser error and font/tab-size cases.

title: Migrate insert panels accordion
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI Accordion, Typography, and expand icon usage in InsertPanels.js.

Acceptance criteria:
- Shape categories expand/collapse and shape insertion/drag behavior is unchanged.
- Existing category and shape presentation remains functional with the new aesthetic.
- The panel remains usable alongside graph/editor panes.

Design notes:
- Use sharp collapsible sections and compact shape tiles; avoid Material accordion styling.

Edge cases:
- Large shape lists, drag from panel to canvas, and narrow pane widths.

Testing strategy:
- Run insert_node spec and manual drag/drop shape insertion.

title: Migrate format drawer controls
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI Drawer, title, divider, icon button, form controls, switches, checkboxes, and theme usage in FormatDrawer.js.

Acceptance criteria:
- Node and edge drawers open/close and call onStyleChange, onColorChange, and onFillColorChange with the same payloads.
- Style switches, style checkboxes, color switch, fillcolor switch, and color pickers preserve IDs and behavior.
- Drawer width/position and graph/editor layout interactions remain equivalent.

Design notes:
- Drawer should feel like a compact inspector panel with clear control grouping and visible state.

Edge cases:
- Default attributes absent, multiple style values, color picker hidden/visible transitions, and node vs edge type differences.

Testing strategy:
- Run insert_node, select/delete, and manual node/edge formatting checks.

title: Migrate export SVG dialog
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI usage in ExportAsSvgDialog.js while preserving SVG filename entry and export behavior.

Acceptance criteria:
- Filename input, cancel, close, and export SVG actions behave as before.
- Existing IDs remain stable.
- Dialog uses app dialog/input/button primitives.

Design notes:
- Keep file action hierarchy clear and compact.

Edge cases:
- Empty filenames, repeated export attempts, and close without export.

Testing strategy:
- Run export SVG coverage and manual browser download behavior if Cypress cannot assert download reliably.

title: Migrate destructive confirmation dialogs
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI usage in DoYouWantToDeleteDialog.js and DoYouWantToReplaceItDialog.js.

Acceptance criteria:
- Cancel/delete and cancel/replace handlers fire exactly as before.
- Dialog IDs, action IDs, and close behavior remain stable.
- Destructive action is visually distinct without changing copy or flow unnecessarily.

Design notes:
- Use the same alert dialog primitive for both confirmations.

Edge cases:
- Nested confirmation launched from an already open dialog must layer and restore focus correctly.

Testing strategy:
- Run browser save/open delete and replace-flow specs.

title: Migrate open-from-browser table dialog
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI dialog/table/sort label/icon button/tooltip usage in OpenFromBrowserDialog.js.

Acceptance criteria:
- Saved graph list renders name, DOT source, timestamp, selection, sorting, opening, and deletion exactly as before.
- Delete action opens the confirmation dialog and preserves selected graph behavior.
- Table headers and row IDs/selectors used by Cypress remain stable.

Design notes:
- Use a high-density table with visible row states and brutalist header affordances.

Edge cases:
- Empty project list, duplicate DOT source, long graph names, long DOT source snippets, and delete while a row is selected.

Testing strategy:
- Run browser_save_and_open and main menu open-flow specs.

title: Migrate save and rename browser dialog flow
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI usage in SaveAsToBrowserDialog.js and preserve the replace-existing graph flow.

Acceptance criteria:
- Name input, cancel, save, close, duplicate-name detection, and replace confirmation behavior are unchanged.
- Dialog IDs and action IDs used by Cypress remain stable.
- The dialog uses app dialog and input primitives.

Design notes:
- Keep copy direct and action hierarchy clear.

Edge cases:
- Empty name, existing name, rename flow, and repeated open/close without losing typed value unexpectedly.

Testing strategy:
- Run browser_save_and_open and main_menu rename/save specs.

title: Migrate export URL dialog
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI usage in ExportAsUrlDialog.js while preserving generated URL, copy, open-link, and cancel behavior.

Acceptance criteria:
- Exported URL content and actions behave exactly as before.
- Copy/open/cancel IDs remain stable.
- Dialog focus and close behavior match the existing workflow.

Design notes:
- URL presentation should be selectable, compact, and clearly separated from actions.

Edge cases:
- Very long DOT sources produce long URLs; ensure layout does not overflow unusably.

Testing strategy:
- Run export_and_import_as_url spec.

title: Migrate dialogs drawers and forms
description:
## Summary
Replace Material UI across settings, storage/export dialogs, confirmation dialogs, help/about overlays, format drawers, insert panels, tables, and form controls.

## Success Criteria
- Dialog, drawer, menu, table, input, select, switch, checkbox, radio, accordion, and action button usage is migrated to Base UI/custom primitives.
- All dialog/drawer IDs, values, callbacks, escape handling, and persistence behavior remain intact.
- Accessibility improves or remains equivalent for labels, focus traps, descriptions, and keyboard navigation.

## Design Notes
- Brutalist/minimal overlays should prioritize hierarchy and speed: dense sections, visible dividers, direct action rows, no card nesting.

## Edge Cases
- Many settings values are persisted as strings; do not normalize types unless existing handlers already do so.

## Testing Strategy
- Run settings, transition, browser save/open, export/import, help, text editor, and format drawer related specs.

title: Migrate settings dialog controls
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI Dialog, form controls, select, switches, radio buttons, inputs, adornments, labels, and helper text in SettingsDialog.js.

Acceptance criteria:
- Engine select, fit graph switch, transition duration, path/shape tween switches, tween precision radio/input/adornment, font size, tab size, and holdoff controls keep current values and handlers.
- Dialog opens/closes by toolbar, menu, close button, and escape as before.
- IDs used by Cypress remain stable.

Design notes:
- Use compact grouped sections with strong labels and dividers.
- Preserve the existing Graph rendering, Graph viewing, and Text Editor grouping.

Edge cases:
- Numeric controls currently pass event.target.value strings.
- Tween precision adornment changes between points and percent.

Testing strategy:
- Run settings, transition, and text_editor specs that modify settings.

title: Replace shell Paper and Grid layout
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI Paper/Grid usage in src/pages/index.js with CSS grid/flex layout that preserves current panes and sizing.

Acceptance criteria:
- Text editor, insert panels, format drawers, and graph canvas occupy the same functional regions as before.
- Existing height calculations for toolbar, padding, snackbar, and fullscreen are preserved or simplified without behavioral change.
- MUI Paper elevation is replaced by tokenized focus/active styling.

Design notes:
- Use visible pane boundaries and a utilitarian layout grid rather than cards or shadows.

Edge cases:
- Fullscreen should remove shell chrome from the graph area as before.
- Updated snackbar open state should not obscure controls or graph content.

Testing strategy:
- Run fullscreen_graph, rendering, text_editor, insert_node, and visual smoke specs.

title: Preserve pane focus and open-state behavior
description:
Summary:
Recreate the current focus and open-state visual behavior without relying on MUI elevation.

Acceptance criteria:
- Text editor, node format drawer, edge format drawer, insert panels, and graph pane still reflect focus/open state.
- setFocus and setFocusIf flows in src/pages/index.js remain functionally unchanged.
- Visual styling communicates focus without shadows/elevation.

Design notes:
- Use border weight, background plane, and high-contrast focus rings instead of elevation.

Edge cases:
- Clicking outside the document and returning should preserve the existing blur/focus semantics.

Testing strategy:
- Manually verify pane focus transitions and run Cypress specs that click editor, graph, insert, and drawers.

title: Rebuild main toolbar with custom controls
description:
Summary:
Replace ButtonAppBar's AppBar, Toolbar, Typography, Button, IconButton, Icon, and MUI icon usage with custom toolbar/button primitives.

Acceptance criteria:
- All current toolbar controls render with the same IDs, aria labels, and handler calls.
- Insert, Node, Edge, and GitHub actions remain visible and usable.
- The title/name area preserves current display behavior.

Design notes:
- Use grouped rectangular controls with clear active/disabled states and no Material elevation.
- Preserve density while making clickable targets reliable.

Edge cases:
- Empty graph name, narrow viewport, disabled undo/redo, and external GitHub link.

Testing strategy:
- Run main_menu, github, undo_redo, pan_and_zoom, insert_node, and smoke toolbar specs.

title: Rebuild main and help menus using Base UI
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI Menu/MenuItem in MainMenu.js and HelpMenu.js with Base UI menu primitives and app styling.

Acceptance criteria:
- Menu items keep current IDs and call the same handlers.
- Menu open/close behavior, anchor positioning, keyboard navigation, escape, and outside click behavior are preserved.
- Main menu and help menu use the same app menu primitive.

Design notes:
- Menus should feel like command lists: compact rows, sharp separators, direct labels.

Edge cases:
- Menu opened from toolbar should restore focus predictably after selection or escape.
- Portaled menu must not appear behind graph SVG or toolbar.

Testing strategy:
- Run main_menu, help, settings, export/import, and keyboard navigation smoke checks.

title: Migrate app shell and navigation
description:
## Summary
Replace Material UI in the root shell, toolbar, menus, and high-level layout while preserving app state and workflow behavior.

## Success Criteria
- src/pages/index.js, withRoot.js, ButtonAppBar.js, MainMenu.js, HelpMenu.js, and root layout styling no longer depend on MUI/tss-react after this epic.
- Toolbar/menu actions call the same handlers with the same data.
- Pane focus/open/fullscreen/update-snackbar layout behavior is unchanged.

## Design Notes
- The shell should read as a dense graph editing instrument: strict grid, compact toolbar groups, visible pane boundaries, and minimal ornament.

## Edge Cases
- Updated snackbar offset, fullscreen graph mode, and focus elevation replacement are high-risk layout states.

## Testing Strategy
- Run main_menu, github, fullscreen_graph, pan_and_zoom, settings open path, help, and smoke layout specs.

title: Replace root theme wrapper with CSS infrastructure
description:
Summary:
Remove the MUI ThemeProvider/CssBaseline path and replace it with app-owned global CSS and root providers needed by Base UI.

Acceptance criteria:
- withRoot.js is removed or reduced to non-MUI concerns.
- Root app layout applies global reset, font variables, color tokens, and portal isolation.
- No behavior in src/pages/index.js changes as part of this task.

Design notes:
- Keep provider surface minimal so component behavior stays visible in the component itself.

Edge cases:
- Ensure portaled overlays appear above graph and editor panes.
- Do not break CRA mounting in src/index.js.

Testing strategy:
- Run npm test target that imports the app and npm run build.

title: Document primitive usage conventions
description:
Summary:
Document how migrated components should use the new primitives and CSS tokens.

Acceptance criteria:
- Notes cover when to use each primitive, how to preserve IDs/aria labels, how to handle portals, and how to avoid behavior changes.
- Notes include examples for a toolbar button, dialog action row, form field, and table row.
- Notes explicitly ban new MUI/tss-react usage.

Design notes:
- Keep guidance implementation-focused and short enough for migration tasks.

Edge cases:
- Include how to handle third-party surfaces like Ace, react-color, and d3-generated SVG.

Testing strategy:
- Use the conventions while migrating the first shell component and revise if they are incomplete.

title: Replace Material icon dependency
description:
Summary:
Choose and apply a non-MUI icon strategy for toolbar, dialog, drawer, and graph controls.

Acceptance criteria:
- All @mui/icons-material imports have an app-owned or alternate-library replacement plan.
- Icons preserve recognizable semantics for new/open/save/undo/redo/zoom/settings/help/close/delete/fullscreen/chevrons.
- Icon sizing aligns with custom toolbar/button primitives.

Design notes:
- Prefer crisp geometric icons that match the brutalist/minimal tone.
- Avoid decorative icons where text is clearer.

Edge cases:
- GitHubIcon currently extends MUI SvgIcon; replace it without changing the GitHub link workflow.

Testing strategy:
- Run Cypress specs that click icon-only controls by ID or aria-label.

title: Add visual smoke coverage for core view states
description:
Summary:
Add lightweight smoke checks for major view states that are likely to break during a ground-up visual rewrite.

Acceptance criteria:
- Smoke coverage opens initial editor, graph fullscreen, settings dialog, open-from-browser dialog with saved data, format drawer, insert panels, and help/about overlays.
- Checks assert presence, sizing sanity, and absence of obvious overlap rather than pixel-perfect snapshots.
- Tests are resilient to the planned aesthetic change.

Design notes:
- Coverage should protect layout integrity without freezing Material UI visuals.

Edge cases:
- Include at least one narrow viewport because toolbar density and portaled overlays are likely to regress there.

Testing strategy:
- Add Cypress smoke checks or extend existing specs with minimal assertions.
- Run the new checks before and after one migrated component to prove they are useful.

title: Document migration notes and release risk
description:
Summary:
Write concise handoff notes after the redesign migration is complete.

Acceptance criteria:
- Notes summarize migrated component families, removed dependencies, new primitives, custom fonts, known residual risks, and exact test commands/results.
- Notes list any intentionally changed selectors or accessible names.
- Notes identify any deferred polish as new Beads issues rather than inline TODOs.

Design notes:
- Keep documentation operational and concrete.

Edge cases:
- If full Cypress could not run locally, document the blocker and targeted coverage that did run.

Testing strategy:
- Confirm notes match git diff, package changes, and test output before final handoff.

title: Re-skin DOT and SVG preview components
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI Card/CardContent/useTheme/tss usage in DotSrcPreview.js and SvgPreview.js.

Acceptance criteria:
- DOT source previews and SVG thumbnails render with the same content and hover interactions.
- Thumbnail event listeners still attach and detach correctly.
- Preview styling uses app CSS tokens and does not rely on MUI theme values.

Design notes:
- Previews should look like flat inspection panes, not Material cards.

Edge cases:
- Missing SVG, malformed SVG, hover enter/leave, and long DOT source.

Testing strategy:
- Run insert panel/preview-related smoke checks and manual hover preview verification.

title: Migrate help mouse keyboard and about dialogs
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI usage in KeyboardShortcutsDialog.js, MouseOperationsDialog.js, AboutDialog.js, and related help entry points.

Acceptance criteria:
- Help menu entries open the correct dialogs.
- Tables/lists preserve current content and close behavior.
- About dialog still displays graphviz/app version details and GitHub/reference links as before.

Design notes:
- Present reference material in dense readable tables with strong headings and no decorative framing.

Edge cases:
- Long shortcut labels, mouse operation descriptions, and version string changes.

Testing strategy:
- Run help and smoke dialog specs.

title: Adapt responsive toolbar and panes
description:
Summary:
Ensure the redesigned shell remains usable on narrow and standard desktop viewports without hiding critical functionality.

Acceptance criteria:
- Toolbar controls wrap, group, or compact predictably without overlapping text or graph/editor panes.
- Dialog triggers and graph controls remain reachable at narrow widths.
- No critical editor workflow is removed on mobile/narrow screens.

Design notes:
- Use compact icon buttons and grouped controls; avoid a marketing-style responsive layout.

Edge cases:
- Long graph names, browser zoom, and small viewport heights.

Testing strategy:
- Run Cypress smoke checks at desktop and narrow viewport sizes.
